user_query:
I'd like to set up a gentle, privacy-respectful routine where you occasionally ask me one thoughtful question to get to know me better over time. Please read input/user_profile.yaml for my timing and boundaries, input/recent_conversations.jsonl to seed follow-up directions, and input/deflections.json to respect anything I've deflected before.

Deliverables:
1) Write output/memory/next-questions.md with exactly two sections: "Question Directions" and "Sensitive Topics". At the very top include a line like "generated_on: YYYY-MM-DD" (today’s date in ISO). Under Question Directions, add 6–10 specific, personalized directions based on recent_conversations.jsonl (each as a bullet with a brief one-line note on what it’s based on). Do not include anything marked sensitive. Under Sensitive Topics, list each deflected topic with either "cooldown until YYYY-MM-DD" (30 days from last deflection) or "permanent skip" (if deflected twice or more). Keep the tone respectful and avoid prying.
2) Write output/schedule.json describing a twice-daily plan honoring the times and timezone in input/user_profile.yaml. Structure it as an array of objects with fields: name, cron_expr, timezone, message. The message should instruct to ask one question woven naturally into the next reply only if it feels appropriate; otherwise skip politely. Use cron_expr values that match the requested times.
3) Write output/prompts_sample.jsonl with 5 natural, one-question messages (one per day for the next week). Tailor them to my interests from recent_conversations.jsonl, avoid sensitive topics and surveillance-like phrasing, keep each under 220 characters, and avoid robotic numbering or checklists.
4) Write output/policy.md (250–400 words) explaining how you’ll decide when to ask, how you’ll handle cooldowns (30 days) and permanent skips (after two deflections), how you’ll track topics, how you’ll skip if I seem busy/stressed, and how I can reschedule or disable this entirely.

Please make sure all files are placed under output/ with the exact paths above.
